executiveSummary.text: XPeng is a Chinese EV maker with credible software and autonomous driving capability, but it remains trapped in a brutal domestic EV price war. At $17.80, with a ~$16.5bn market cap, the stock sits closer to the bottom of its 52-week range than the top. The core debate is simple: can XPeng translate strong product and software into durable gross margin expansion before the market loses patience? Bottom line: HOLD — SPECULATIVE. Interesting technology, weak industry structure, no profits. Worth w

businessModel.text: XPeng designs and sells premium EVs in China and is increasingly positioning itself as a software-defined vehicle business. Revenue comes from vehicle sales plus smaller contributions from services and software features. The long-term bull case is not just EV volume, it is software monetisation and autonomous driving differentiation.

financialSnapshot.text: Price: $17.80 Market cap: $16.5bn 52-week range: $15.38 – $28.24 P/E: N/A EPS: -$0.60 Beta: 1.25

recentCatalysts.text: China EV competition remains intense, compressing industry margins. Any evidence of improving delivery growth and gross margin would matter. Autonomous driving and software partnerships remain the strategic upside lever.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case is that XPeng survives and grows, but profitability remains delayed. Bull case requires strong software monetisation and margin recovery. Bear case is continued price competition and capital destruction.

keyRisks.text: 1 China EV price war continues longer than expected. 2 Geopolitical and ADR risk. 3 No clear path to sustained profitability.

risks.risk: 1 China EV price war continues longer than expected.
3 No clear path to sustained profitability.

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: speculative growth investors who want China EV exposure. Should avoid: anyone wanting clean earnings visibility.

recommendation.text: HOLD — SPECULATIVE. Better than many peers operationally, but still not a high-quality industry. Entry / Exit: Below $16 gets more interesting. Above $24 starts to look stretched without a margin turn. Conviction Trend Latest conviction: 58/100 .
